words.definition: to make a process or system operate using machines or computers instead of people
words.example: The factory automated its packaging line to cut labor costs.

words.definition: to make a system or process simpler and more efficient
words.example: The new software streamlined the entire deployment pipeline.
words.usage_note: Avoid pairing with 'complicate' in the same sentence.

words.definition: to combine two or more systems or components so they work together as one
words.example: We integrated the payment gateway directly into the checkout flow.

words.definition: to carefully organize and coordinate a complex process or set of systems
words.example: Kubernetes orchestrates hundreds of containers across the cluster automatically.
words.usage_note: In tech contexts this usually refers to coordinating systems, not people.

words.definition: able to be changed in size or scale, especially able to handle growth without breaking down
words.example: The architecture needs to be scalable enough to handle ten times the current traffic.

words.definition: to set up and allocate the resources or infrastructure a system needs before it runs
words.example: The script automatically provisions new servers when demand spikes.
words.usage_note: Mostly used as a technical/IT term, not in general conversation.

words.definition: to assign a task or responsibility to another person, system, or process
words.example: The main thread delegates heavy computation to background workers.

words.definition: to make a process happen faster or more efficiently
words.example: Automating the approval step expedited the entire release cycle.
words.usage_note: Sounds overly formal in casual speech — prefer 'speed up' there.

words.definition: able to function or sustain itself without needing outside help or intervention
words.example: The pipeline was designed to be self-sufficient, requiring no manual restarts.

words.definition: duplicated as a backup so that a system continues working if one part fails
words.example: Critical services run on redundant servers in case one instance goes down.
words.usage_note: In tech this is a positive/neutral term, unlike its everyday meaning of 'unnecessary'.
